article_title: FDA Grants Sonrotoclax Accelerated Approval as First BCL-2 Inhibitor for Relapsed/Refractory Mantle Cell Lymphoma
summary: Submission approvable with 2 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid FDA-approval coverage. Every clinical specific verified verbatim across 6 reachable snapshots (MedCity validated separately via WebFetch). Verified: May 13, 2026 approval date, R/R MCL ≥2 prior lines including BTKi label, 320 mg once-daily dose, four-week ramp-up (ONS); BGB-11417-201 + NCT05471843 trial identifier, 103-patient single-arm Phase 1/2 design, 52% ORR with 95% CI 42-62, 1.9-month median time to response, 15.8-month median DoR with 95% CI 7.4-NE, 11.9-month median follow-up, 115-patient safety population, 37% serious adverse reactions, pneumonia 10% (ASCO Post); 7% TLS at recommended ramp-up, 14% serious infections with 17% Grade 3-4 + 2.6% fatal, 18% Grade 3-4 neutropenia with 6% Grade 4, fast track designation (BioSpace press release); Michael Wang 'foundational therapy' quote (HealthTree); $100M Leerink Beqalzi MCL 2030 sales estimate verbatim 'Leerink Partners models Beqalzi will reach about $100 million in sales in the MCL indication by 2030', 4-6h vs 26h half-life comparison verbatim 'It's also designed with a short half-life — four to six hours versus about 26 hours for Venclexta', Basel Switzerland HQ, off-label venetoclax in MCL framing (MedCity, verified via WebFetch since snapshot returned 403); CELESTIAL-RRMCL NCT06742996 confirmatory trial + 149 locations across 17 countries + 16% CR rate + 'first BCL-2 inhibitor' framing (Pharmaceutical Technology, Archive.org fallback). FDA primary press release cited as source-0 (Rule 9 compliance).

source-0.status: Primary publication (Rule 9): FDA approval announcement. Confirms sonrotoclax + accelerated approval + mantle cell lymphoma framing.

source-1.status: Oncology Nursing Society. Verifies May 13 dateline, BTK inhibitor + ≥2 lines indication, 320 mg once-daily dose, four-week ramp-up.

source-2.status: ASCO Post. Verifies BGB-11417-201 / NCT05471843, 103 patients, 52% ORR, 1.9-month TTR, 15.8-month DoR, 11.9-month follow-up, 115 safety population, 37% serious AE, 10% pneumonia.

source-3.status: BioSpace republication of BeOne press release. Verifies 7% TLS, 14% serious infections / 17% Gr 3-4 / 2.6% fatal, 18% Gr 3-4 neutropenia / 6% Gr 4, fast track designation.

source-4.status: HealthTree (patient advocacy / oncology community outlet). Verifies Michael Wang 'foundational therapy' quote verbatim.

source-5.status: MedCity News: bot-blocked, snapshot failed. Verified directly via WebFetch — Leerink $100M 2030 forecast, 4-6h vs 26h half-life comparison, Basel HQ, off-label venetoclax framing all verbatim in the live page.

source-6.status: Pharmaceutical Technology (via Archive.org). Verifies CELESTIAL-RRMCL NCT06742996 confirmatory trial, 149 locations across 17 countries, 16% CR rate, 'first BCL-2' framing.

editor_notes.content_quality: News-category (well within range), Overview / Trial and Efficacy / Dosing and Mechanism / Confirmatory Trial / Reactions / What We Don't Know structure. Clinical, precise, neutral tone. Properly notes accelerated-approval requires confirmatory verification, names the trial, distinguishes the 103-patient efficacy population from the 115-patient safety population.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the May 13 sonrotoclax FDA approval. Note: this is the primary submission in a triple-overlap cluster (PR #1318 and #1321 also cover the same approval). Recommend REJECT for the two duplicates per v3.9.0 policy.
concerns: Triple-duplicate cluster: PRs #1312 (this one), #1318, and #1321 all cover the same FDA Sonrotoclax accelerated approval. This submission is the primary; the other two should be REJECTED as duplicates.
recommendations: Add ons.org, ascopost.com, healthtree.org, medcitynews.com, pharmaceutical-technology.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — all standard outlets for oncology regulatory coverage.

content_preview.title: FDA Grants Sonrotoclax Accelerated Approval as First BCL-2 Inhibitor for Relapsed/Refractory Mantle Cell Lymphoma
content_preview.summary: BeOne Medicines' Beqalzi clears the FDA on a 52% overall response rate in 103 post-BTK patients, becoming the first BCL-2 inhibitor specifically approved for MCL and setting up a head-to-head with AbbVie and Roche's off-label Venclexta.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration granted accelerated approval to sonrotoclax (Beqalzi) on May 13, 2026, clearing BeOne Medicines' next-generation BCL-2 inhibitor for adults with relapsed or refractory mantle cell lymphoma (MCL) who have already received at least two lines of systemic therapy, including a Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitor, [the Oncology Nursing Society reported](https://www.ons.org/publications-research/voice/news-views/05-2026/fda-grants-accelerated-appr...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically
